content_markdown: Which of the following graphs shows the relationship between current $I$ and voltage $V$ for a filament lamp?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs labeled A, B, C, and D showing current I on the y-axis and voltage V on the x-axis. Graph A is a horizontal line. Graph B is a straight line through the origin. Graph C is a curve with increasing gradient. Graph D is a curve with decreasing gradient.

vibe_explanation: As the voltage across a filament lamp increases, the current increases, which causes the temperature of the filament to rise. This increase in temperature leads to an increase in resistance ($R = V/I$). On an $I-V$ graph, the resistance is the reciprocal of the gradient ($1/R$). Therefore, as resistance increases, the gradient of the $I-V$ curve must decrease, resulting in the characteristic curve shown in graph D.
